source: state DOR 2025 rate schedules / instructions (per-state source_url)

AK.notes: no state tax on wage income

AL.notes: Rates 2% to 5%. Head of household uses the same rate schedule as single, so single's values are copied. SIMPLIFICATIONS: (1) Alabama allows a deduction for federal income tax paid, which materially lowers taxable income and is NOT modeled here, so computed tax is overstated. (2) The standard deduction actually phases down with AGI (single from $3,000 toward a $2,500 floor, MFJ from $8,500 toward $4,000); the maximum is used here. (3) Dependent exemptions not modeled. Personal exemption is a deduction, not a credit.

AR.notes: Top rate 3.9% (cut retroactive to 2024, unchanged for 2025). SIMPLIFICATION: Arkansas actually uses three separate net-income tax tables plus a 'bracket adjustment' that smooths the cliff between tables; this is collapsed to a two-bracket schedule (2% then 3.9%) approximating the middle/upper-income table. The $29 per-filer personal exemption is a CREDIT ($29 single/HoH, $58 MFJ). Standard deduction is inflation-indexed ($2,410 single / $4,820 MFJ for 2025).

AZ.notes: Flat 2.5% (since 2023). Arizona's standard deduction equals the federal standard deduction; values shown are the 2025 federal amounts as amended by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act ($15,750 single / $31,500 MFJ / $23,625 HoH). Arizona has no personal exemption (replaced by per-dependent credits, not modeled). Optional 25% charitable boost to the standard deduction not modeled.

CA.notes: 2025 tax-year FTB Schedules X/Y/Z (inflation-indexed). Base rates 1% to 12.3%; the 1% Mental Health Services Tax on taxable income over $1,000,000 is folded into the schedule as an added 1% bracket at $1,000,000, giving a 13.3% top marginal rate (same $1M threshold for all statuses). Personal exemption is a CREDIT ($153 single/HoH, $306 MFJ) subtracted from tax, not a deduction. Dependent exemption credits and itemized deductions not modeled.

CO.notes: Flat 4.40% statutory rate for 2025 (temporary TABOR-refund rate reductions in some years are not modeled). Colorado taxable income starts from federal taxable income, so the federal standard deduction applies; values shown are the 2025 federal amounts as amended by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act ($15,750 single / $31,500 MFJ / $23,625 HoH). No state personal exemption. State addbacks/subtractions and the itemized-deduction cap not modeled.

CT.notes: Rates 2% to 6.99% (two lowest rates cut to 2%/4.5% effective 2024; unchanged for 2025). Connecticut has NO standard deduction (set to 0). The personal exemption is a deduction that phases out above set CT-AGI thresholds ($15,000 single, $24,000 MFJ, $19,000 HoH at the maximum); the phase-out, the 3% phase-out add-back, the tax-recapture provisions, and the personal tax credit are not modeled, so tax at higher incomes is slightly understated.

DC.notes: DC graduated 2025 schedule, 7 brackets, top marginal rate 10.75% over $1,000,000. Same bracket schedule for all filing statuses (HOH uses the single schedule). DC has no personal exemption (repealed for 2018+). Standard deduction conforms to the federal 2025 amounts as amended (single $15,750, MFJ $31,500, HOH $23,625).

DE.notes: Rates 0% to 6.6%; the first $2,000 of taxable income is exempt (0% bracket). Delaware uses the same rate schedule for all filing statuses (brackets are NOT doubled for MFJ). The $110 per-filer personal exemption is a CREDIT against tax ($110 single/HoH, $220 MFJ for two filers). Additional/dependent personal credits and the extra credit for age 60+ not modeled.

FL.notes: no state tax on wage income

GA.notes: Georgia flat tax. HB111 (signed April 2025) cut the 2025 rate to 5.19% retroactive to Jan 1, 2025 (down from the previously scheduled 5.39%). Standard deduction (2025, unchanged from 2024): single/HOH $12,000, MFJ $24,000. Personal exemption for taxpayer/spouse was repealed under HB1437; a $4,000-per-dependent exemption remains but is not modeled here.

HI.notes: Hawaii graduated, 12 brackets, top marginal rate 11%. Brackets widened for tax year 2025 by Act 46 (SLH 2024); MFJ thresholds are 2x single, HOH thresholds are 1.5x single (Schedule III). 2025 standard deduction (unchanged from 2024 per Act 46): single $4,400, MFJ $8,800, HOH $6,424. Personal exemption $1,144 per exemption, taken as a deduction (single 1, MFJ 2, HOH 1).

IA.notes: Iowa transitioned to a flat 3.8% tax for tax year 2025 (no more brackets). Iowa now follows the federal standard deduction (2025 amended amounts: single $15,750, MFJ $31,500, HOH $23,625). Iowa retains a $40 personal exemption CREDIT per personal credit, subtracted from tax (single 1 credit = $40; MFJ and HOH 2 credits = $80). Additional age/blindness credits not modeled.

ID.notes: Idaho flat tax. HB40 (signed March 2025) cut the 2025 rate to 5.3% retroactive to Jan 1, 2025 (down from 5.695% in 2024). Idaho conforms to the federal standard deduction (2025 amended amounts: single $15,750, MFJ $31,500, HOH $23,625). Idaho's schedule technically taxes the first $4,811 (single) / $9,622 (MFJ) of Idaho taxable income at 0% before the 5.3% rate; that zero bracket is not modeled here, so tax is slightly overstated for low taxable incomes.

IL.notes: Illinois flat tax 4.95%. No standard deduction. Personal exemption allowance $2,850 per person for 2025 (up from $2,775), taken as a deduction (single 1, MFJ 2, HOH 1). The exemption is disallowed for taxpayers with AGI over $250,000 single / $500,000 MFJ; that phase-out is not modeled here.

IN.notes: Indiana flat state rate 3.00% for 2025 (down from 3.05% in 2024). No standard deduction. Personal/dependent exemption $1,000 per person, taken as a deduction (single 1, MFJ 2, HOH 1). Indiana counties levy additional local income taxes that are not modeled here.

KS.notes: Two-bracket graduated schedule under SB 1 (2024): 5.2% then 5.58% on income above $23,000 (single/HOH/MFS) or $46,000 (MFJ). Standard deduction and a large personal exemption ($9,160 single/HOH; $18,320 MFJ) both apply as deductions; additional $2,320 per dependent is not modeled.

KY.notes: Flat 4.0% for tax year 2025 (down from 4.5% in 2024; scheduled to fall to 3.5% in 2026). Standard deduction is $3,270 for 2025 and applies per return regardless of filing status; Kentucky has no personal exemption.

LA.notes: 2025 reform (special session Nov 2024) replaced graduated 1.85%/3.5%/4.25% brackets with a flat 3.0% rate effective Jan 1, 2025. Combined personal-exemption/standard-deduction is $12,500 (single/MFS) and $25,000 (MFJ/QSS/HOH); prior personal and dependent exemptions were eliminated.

MA.notes: Flat 5.0% on most income plus a 4% surtax (the voter-approved 'millionaires tax') on taxable income over $1,083,150 for 2025, modeled as a second bracket at 9.0%. Massachusetts has no standard deduction; personal exemption is $4,400 (single), $8,800 (MFJ), $6,800 (HOH).

MD.notes: STATE income tax only; county/local income tax (up to 3.30%) is a separate dataset and excluded. The Budget Reconciliation and Financing Act of 2025 added two new top brackets effective tax year 2025: 6.25% and 6.50% (top marginal is 6.50%, above $1,000,000 single / $1,200,000 MFJ), on top of the retained schedule through 5.75%. Standard deduction is $3,350 (single/MFS) and $6,700 (MFJ/HOH); the income-based phase-in was eliminated. Personal exemption base $3,200 per filer (phases down above $100,000 AGI; not modeled). HOH/QSS use the joint bracket schedule.

ME.notes: Three-bracket graduated schedule for 2025 (5.8% / 6.75% / 7.15%), thresholds inflation-adjusted per Maine Revenue Services 2025 rate schedules. Personal exemption is $5,150 per filer ($10,300 for MFJ). Standard deduction and exemption phase out at high AGI (not modeled here).

MI.notes: Flat 4.25% for tax year 2025. Michigan has no standard deduction; personal exemption is $5,800 per person for 2025 ($11,600 for MFJ). Dependent exemptions are not modeled.

MN.notes: 2025 MN DoR inflation-adjusted brackets (top rate 9.85%). Standard deduction single $14,950 / MFJ $29,900 / HoH $22,500. MN has no personal exemption for the filer (only a $5,200 per-dependent exemption, not modeled here).

MO.notes: 2025 MO graduated schedule, top rate 4.7% (reduced from 4.8% on 1/1/2025). First $1,313 of taxable income exempt (0% bracket); same bracket schedule for all filing statuses. Standard deduction conforms to federal 2025 amounts (single $15,750 / MFJ $31,500 / HoH $23,625). No personal exemption (MO exemption is $0).

MS.notes: 2025 MS flat rate 4.4% (Build Up Mississippi Act, HB1). MS taxes taxable income above $10,000 at 4.4% (first $10,000 of taxable income is a 0% bracket). Modeled as flat with the $10,000 zero-bracket folded into standard_deduction on top of the statutory standard deduction (single $2,300 / MFJ $4,600 / HoH $3,400), so standard_deduction shown = statutory std + $10,000. personal_exemption is the statutory MS exemption (single $6,000 / MFJ $12,000 / HoH $8,000). Effective exempt floor: single $18,300, MFJ $26,600, HoH $21,400.

MT.notes: 2025 MT two-rate ordinary-income schedule (4.7% then top 5.9%), from official 2025 Form 2 instructions rate tables. 5.9% threshold: single/MFS $21,100, MFJ $42,200, HoH $31,700. Standard deduction conforms to federal 2025 amounts (single $15,750 / MFJ $31,500 / HoH $23,625). No personal exemption. (Net long-term capital gains are taxed at a lower 3%/4.1% schedule, not modeled here for wage income.)

NC.notes: 2025 North Carolina flat individual income tax rate of 4.25% (down from 4.50% in 2024; scheduled to fall to 3.99% in 2026). No personal exemption; standard deduction varies by filing status.

ND.notes: 2025 North Dakota individual income tax; first bracket taxed at 0%, top marginal rate 2.5%. ND computes tax on federal taxable income, so the standard_deduction values here are the 2025 federal standard deductions (Single $15,000 / MFJ $30,000 / HoH $22,500) used to approximate ND taxable income from gross income.

NE.notes: 2025 NE graduated schedule, top rate 5.20%. Standard deduction single $8,600 / MFJ $17,200 / HoH $12,600. Personal exemption is a nonrefundable CREDIT of $171 per exemption (single/HoH $171, MFJ $342 for two filers). Single and MFJ bracket thresholds from official 2025 NE booklet / Tax Foundation; HoH thresholds derived from the official 2025 NE Tax Table (matches booklet cumulative tax of $3,276 at $77,760).

NH.notes: no state tax on wage income

NJ.notes: 2025 NJ gross income tax, top rate 10.75%. NJ has NO standard deduction. Personal exemption $1,000 per filer taken as a deduction ($2,000 MFJ). Single/MFS use Rate Schedule I; MFJ, HoH, and qualifying surviving spouse use Rate Schedule II (the schedule with 2.45% and 3.5% mid brackets). HoH uses Schedule II with a single $1,000 exemption.

NM.notes: 2025 NM PIT restructured by HB 252, top rate 5.9%. Standard deduction conforms to federal 2025 amounts (single $15,750 / MFJ $31,500 / HoH $23,625). No personal exemption (NM offers a $4,000 dependent deduction and low-income credits, not modeled). HoH and married-filing-jointly share the same rate schedule.

NV.notes: no state tax on wage income

NY.notes: 2025 NY State personal income tax (Article 22) rate schedule; state tax only, excludes NYC and Yonkers local income taxes (separate dataset). Standard deduction per filing status; NY grants no personal exemption for the filer (only $1,000 per dependent), so personal_exemption=0. Top marginal rate 10.9% on income over $25,000,000 (increased rates in effect through tax year 2027).

OH.notes: 2025 Ohio individual income tax on nonbusiness income; income up to $26,050 exempt (0%), then 2.75%, with a top marginal rate of 3.125% over $100,000 (rate reduced by HB 96, June 2025; collapses to flat 2.75% in 2026). Ohio has no standard deduction; the bracket schedule is identical for all filing statuses. Personal exemption modeled at the base $2,400 per filer ($4,800 MFJ); the actual exemption is income-tiered ($2,400 if OAGI<=$40,000, $2,150 if $40,000-$80,000, $1,850 if >$80,000).

OK.notes: 2025 Oklahoma individual income tax; six graduated brackets with a top marginal rate of 4.75%. Standard deduction by filing status; personal exemption $1,000 per filer (deduction). Head of household uses the married-filing-jointly (doubled) bracket schedule per the OK Form 511 tax tables.

OR.notes: 2025 Oregon individual income tax; four graduated brackets with a top marginal rate of 9.9%. Head of household uses the married-filing-jointly bracket schedule. Oregon's personal exemption is a credit ($256 per filer, $512 MFJ), so exemption_is_credit=true; the credit phases out at higher federal AGI (above roughly $100,000 single / $200,000 joint), which is not modeled here.

PA.notes: 2025 Pennsylvania flat individual income tax rate of 3.07% on taxable compensation. Pennsylvania allows no standard deduction and no personal exemption (a separate Tax Forgiveness credit exists for low-income filers but is not modeled here).

RI.notes: RI uses the same bracket thresholds for all filing statuses. Top marginal rate 5.99% (over $181,650). Standard deduction ($10,900 single / $21,800 MFJ / $16,350 HoH) and the $5,100-per-person personal exemption both phase out to $0 for higher RI AGI (roughly above $246,000); the base amounts are used here.

SC.notes: SC begins from FEDERAL taxable income (SC1040 Line 1), so it has no separate state standard deduction; the federal 2025 standard deduction ($15,000 single / $30,000 MFJ / $22,500 HoH) is used here to approximate SC taxable income from gross. SC has no personal exemption (only a dependent exemption). 2025 top marginal rate is 6% per SCDOR (down from 6.2% in 2024); brackets: 0% to $3,560, 3% to $17,830, 6% above.

SD.notes: no state tax on wage income

TN.notes: no state tax on wage income

TX.notes: no state tax on wage income

UT.notes: Utah is a flat tax. HB106 (signed 2025-03, retroactive to 2025-01-01) cut the rate from 4.55% to 4.50% for tax year 2025 (the 4.55% figure in the build request was the 2024 rate). Utah has no standard deduction; instead a taxpayer tax credit (equal to 6% of federal deductions/exemptions) phases out at 1.3% of income above status-based thresholds. That credit is not modeled here (standard_deduction=0, personal_exemption=0), so this record slightly overstates tax at low-to-moderate incomes.

VA.notes: VA uses the same bracket thresholds for all filing statuses; top rate 5.75% begins at only $17,000. VA has no separate head-of-household filing status, so HoH uses the single standard deduction ($8,750) and exemption. 2025 standard deduction increased to $8,750 single / $17,500 MFJ. Personal exemption $930 per person.

VT.notes: Official 2025 VT rate schedules (Schedules X/Y-1/Z). Top marginal rate 8.75%. VT standard deduction 2025: $7,650 single / $15,300 MFJ / $11,450 HoH; personal exemption $5,300 per person. Thresholds shown are the marginal-rate change points; VT instructs taxable income under $75,000 to use tax tables.

WA.notes: no state tax on wage income

WI.notes: Top marginal rate 7.65%. WI head-of-household uses the single-filer bracket schedule. WI's standard deduction is a sliding-scale amount that phases DOWN to $0 as income rises (fully phased out around $128k single / $155k MFJ); the 2025 MAXIMUM standard deduction is used here ($13,560 single / $25,110 MFJ / $17,520 HoH per the Form 1 Standard Deduction Table), so this record understates tax at higher incomes. Personal exemption $700 per person.

WV.notes: SB2033 (effective 2025-01-01) lowered WV rates from 2.36%-5.12% to 2.22%-4.82% while keeping the same bracket structure; top marginal rate 4.82% (over $60,000). WV has NO standard deduction; it allows a personal exemption of $2,000 per exemption ($4,000 for MFJ with two exemptions). Single/MFJ/HoH share the same thresholds (only married-filing-separately halves them). Brackets cross-checked against Tax Foundation 2025.

WY.notes: no state tax on wage income
